//: Native-localization-review policy. Copy to your repo root as localization.config.json. HOW strings load lives in the adapter; WHAT to enforce lives here.
//layers: Optional per-layer metadata: where Stage 5 writes fixes. Especially important for codegen'd files whose source-of-truth is a different file.

layers.name: generatedExample
layers.//note: AUTO-GENERATED layer: edit the source, then regenerate — never hand-edit the generated file. Wire the generator's --check mode into CI to gate staleness.

//allowlist: Suppresses false 'untranslated' hits. A BARE value is do-not-translate in every locale (brand/acronym); a 'locale:value' entry is a cognate correct in ONE locale only, so it isn't flagged without masking a real gap elsewhere.
//placeholderEquivalents: Groups of interchangeable runtime-supplied placeholders. Common case: a generator supplies pre-declined forms of a name (nominative/genitive/accusative/…) and each locale picks the grammatical case its language needs — correct, NOT drift. Each group collapses to one token before comparison.
//coverageIgnoreKeyPatterns: Keys a locale may legitimately lack — skip ONLY the coverage check. The entry below is the i18next plural-suffix preset (Japanese has no `_one` form; that's correct, not a gap). All other checks still run on keys that exist.
//ledger: Decisions ledger (JSONL, one line per resolved finding) — makes re-runs incremental, persists human overrides, and accumulates tie-break stats for styleSpec promotion. Commit it with the fixes.
//styleSpec: Makes 'spec'-class findings decidable so nothing is human-only (see SKILL.md → Decision procedure, incl. the INFERENCE GUARD: the inferred spec is emitted for a one-time eyeball, cross-checked against per-language pitfall notes, and suppressed for a locale whose corpus Stage 1 flags too heavily). infer:true bootstraps each locale's register/termbase/punctuation/number/length/filler conventions from the shipped corpus. tieBreak is the genuine-preference default. perLocale holds the hand-set brand overrides that always outrank inference — Stage-4 human overrides get written back here (and to the ledger).

rules: prefer genitive over three-noun compounds in headings
de.//: override an inferred value; one call resolves many downstream findings

termbase.//: canonical target per concept; a locale rendering it differently is a 'consistency' change, not taste

liveCheck.//: Stage 3 spot-check via /browse. Render locally (your dev server) before shipping.
